findings.summary: Assumed widened and isolated test targets would reveal only product regressions; in practice the kernel and integration suites depended on ambient intent/plugin registration and had to be made self-contained first.
findings.evidence: Initial `bb check:kernel` failed with 15 failures in `test/kernel/state_machine_test.cljc`; initial `bb test:int` failed with 65 failures until explicit bootstrap was added in `test/kernel/state_machine_test.cljc` and `test/integration/fixtures.cljc`.
findings.proposed_fix: Add a rule for harness work: when creating a kernel-only target or widening a suite, first make tests self-contained by registering intents/plugins explicitly. Keep reusable bootstrap fixtures for integration/kernel tiers.

findings.summary: Used BSD `pgrep` with a Linux-style mental model (`pgrep -c claude` / `pgrep -c -f claude`), which returned usage text and wasted early turns.
findings.evidence: Commands `pgrep -c claude` and `pgrep -c -f claude` both returned BSD `pgrep` usage output at session start.
findings.proposed_fix: Update local rules/skills to use portable process counting on macOS: `pgrep claude | wc -l` or rely on `~/.claude/active-agents.json` instead of `pgrep -c`.

findings.summary: Dispatched `model-review` with a context bundle above the skill's preferred size, triggering avoidable size warnings on every axis.
findings.evidence: `model-review.py` warned: `arch context 17501 bytes > 15KB`, `formal context 17501 bytes > 15KB`, `mechanical context 17501 bytes > 15KB`.
findings.proposed_fix: Add a pre-dispatch byte-count gate to the `model-review` workflow: if context exceeds 15KB, force one more summarization pass before llmx dispatch.

findings.summary: Used a read-only zsh variable name while probing test output, causing an avoidable shell failure.
findings.evidence: Command `bb test >/tmp/evo-bb-test.log 2>&1; status=$?; ...` failed with `zsh:1: read-only variable: status`.
findings.proposed_fix: Add a shell rule for zsh sessions: never assign to `status`; use `rc=$?` or inspect the exit code inline.
